channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Liberty hope their last-second shot provides a new outcome, a WNBA title, rather than 1999's letdown
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — It's been 25 years since the New York Liberty had a chance to close out a WNBA championship. Just like in 1999, a last-second shot gave them the opportunity to play for a title. This time around, the Liberty are hoping for a different outcome.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: PGA of America director defends record-high Ryder Cup prices at Bethpage Black: 'Tier 1 event'
item.description: Ticket prices for the Ryder Cup at Bethpage Black next year are historically high, angering fans; but the PGA of America says it is justified.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Leonard won't play for Los Angeles Clippers to start the season while rehabbing knee, report says
item.description: INGLEWOOD, Calif. (AP) — Kawhi Leonard won’t play for the Los Angeles Clippers to start the season because of ongoing rehabilitation from inflammation in his right knee, according to a report. Citing unidentified sources, ESPN reported Thursday that Leonard will be sidelined indefinitely.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: All-Star 1B Freddie Freeman out of Dodgers' lineup for Game 4 of NLCS against Mets
item.description: All-Star first baseman Freddie Freeman is out of the Los Angeles Dodgers’ starting lineup for Game 4 of the National League Championship Series against the New York Mets.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Emma Hayes names USWNT roster for first matches since winning gold at the Olympics
item.description: Trinity Rodman will miss a trio of upcoming matches for the U.S. women's national soccer team as she continues to recover from a back injury. Coach Emma Hayes named her roster Thursday for games against Iceland on Oct. 24 in Austin, Texas, and Oct. 27 in Nashville.
item.dc:creator: ANNE M. PETERSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: South Florida Athletics named a 'Best College Athletic Department' by Front Office Sports
item.description: TAMPA (USF) - University of South Florida Athletics has been named one of Front Office Sports’ “Best College Athletic Departments” for 2024.
item.dc:creator: Brian Siegrist, USF, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada women's volleyball captain slams trans athletes in her sport as team decides against players’ refusal
item.description: University of Nevada women's volleyball captain Sia Liillii spoke out against her own school amid a dispute over whether to forfeit a match.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Angel Reese recalls Caitlin Clark trash talk changing her life: 'It’s just a full-circle moment'
item.description: Chicago Sky star Angel Reese recalled the moment that changed her life, when she went up against Caitlin Clark and Iowa in the national championship in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Boise State RB Jeanty a unanimous AP midseason All-America pick, Colorado's Hunter fills 2 positions
item.description: Boise State running back Ashton Jeanty was a unanimous selection and Colorado two-way star Travis Hunter occupies two first-team positions on The Associated Press midseason All-America team released Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC OLSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ahead of 49ers vs. Chiefs, here’s how teams fared in regular-season Super Bowl rematches
item.description: When the 49ers play the Chiefs on Sunday, it will mark the 10th time that there has been a regular-season rematch of the previous season's Super Bowl teams
item.dc:creator: Justice delos Santos, The Mercury News

item.title: Chargers' Jim Harbaugh says cardiologist told him he has 'the heart of an athlete' following medical scare
item.description: Los Angeles Chargers head coach Jim Harbaugh said his doctors told him that he has "the heart of an athlete" following his medical scare during the Chargers last game.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Heat's Tyler Herro gets distracted as fans fight in stands after game: 'This is crazy'
item.description: Miami Heat star guard Tyler Herro tried to talk about his team's performance after Tuesday night's game, but he was distracted by a fight in the stands.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ex-NBA star doesn't think Lakers are 'serious anymore' after low-key offseason, drafting Bronny James
item.description: Former NBA star DeMarcus Cousins was critical of the Los Angeles Lakers' offseason decision-making on Tuesday after the team brought in Bronny James.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 76ers' Jared McCain hospitalized after scary fall during preseason game
item.description: Philadelphia 76ers guard Jared McCain was caught up in a scary moment on Wednesday night in a preseason game against the Brooklyn Nets.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Liberty's Sabrina Ionescu nails long 3-pointer to help team to Game 3 win in WNBA Finals
item.description: New York Liberty star Sabrina Ionescu delivered one of the biggest shots in franchise history as she nailed a deep 3-pointer to win Game 3 in the WNBA Finals.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dodgers lead Mets 2-1 ahead of NLCS Game 4
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — On a night of misplayed comebackers, the New York Mets put themselves in a need of yet another comeback. Luis Severino twice botched grounders, leading to a pair of unearned runs in the second inning.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: After Severino misplays a pair of comebackers, Mets in need of NLCS comeback against Dodgers
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — On a night of misplayed comebackers, the New York Mets put themselves in a need of yet another comeback. Luis Severino twice botched grounders, leading to a pair of unearned runs in the second inning.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A German in charge of England? Nationality matters less than it used to in international soccer
item.description: The question was inevitable. At his first news conference as England’s newly appointed head coach, Thomas Tuchel – a German – was asked on Wednesday what message he had for fans who would have preferred an Englishman in charge of their beloved national team.
item.dc:creator: STEVE DOUGLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lonzo Ball makes long-awaited return from knee injuries, scores 10 in Bulls' preseason game
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — When the moment finally arrived, after multiple knee surgeries and one grueling rehab, Lonzo Ball raised his right arm and blew a kiss to the cheering United Center crowd. Fans rose to their feet as the announcement echoed through the arena: “Checking in for the Chicago Bulls! No.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Giants' woes continue with loss of Andrew Thomas
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Things have taken a massive downturn for the Giants. The Giants are 2 and 4 and now they have lost their franchise tackle Andrew Thomas for the season after undergoing surgery for a Lisfranc injury on his left foot.
item.dc:creator: Marc Malusis, PIX 11

item.title: NWSL expansion team BOS Nation FC apologizes for 'Too Many Balls' campaign
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The National Women’s Soccer League expansion team in Boston apologized on Wednesday for a “Too Many Balls” marketing campaign that draw a harsh reaction from the transgender community and others.
item.dc:creator: JIMMY GOLEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CMU football aims to win fourth straight conference game on homecoming
item.description: It's time for CMU homecoming and the Cowboys of New Mexico Highlands come in confident following their first win of the season
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: The Super Bowl will return to Atlanta's Mercedes-Benz Stadium in 2028
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — The Super Bowl will return to Atlanta in 2028 at the home of the Falcons, following a vote of approval by NFL team owners on Tuesday at the league's annual fall meeting.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: BYU hopes to finish strong after 6-0 start
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - From being picked 13th in the Big 12 Conference, to be ranked 13th in the nation, BYU is off to a 6-0 start for just the sixth time in school history without any superstars or sure-fire NFL prospects. In fact, 16 different players have scored touchdowns this year.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Dodgers' Flaherty shares NLCS Game 1 'moment' with mother
item.description: The Los Angeles Dodgers' major trade-deadline acquisition from this summer is already worth it. Jack Flaherty was the star of the show in Game 1 of the NLCS between L.A. and the New York Mets, as he pitched an electric seven shutout innings while giving up just two hits in a 9-0 win.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Los Angeles Clippers to make Intuit Dome debut Monday
item.description: For the first time in one quarter of a century, the Los Angeles Clippers will play a home game in their own, exclusive arena on Monday night. That's because the Intuit Dome, the $2 billion Inglewood arena, is now open seven years after it was first announced to be in the works.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: North Carolina football player dies from rare lung cancer at 23
item.description: UNC had honored Tylee Craft, 23, during Saturday's game. Coach Mack Brown, however, said he hadn't learned of Craft's death until after the game.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Broncos fall to Chargers 23-16
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)— The Denver Broncos' three-game winning streak came to an end with a 23-16 loss to the Los Angeles Chargers at Empower Field. The Broncos struggled early, trailing 23-0 before attempting a late comeback that ultimately fell short.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Mets add McNeil to NLCS roster while Dodgers drop Vesia and Rojas, add Kiermaier and Honeywell
item.description: Jeff McNeil was added to the New York Mets' roster for the NL Championship Series against the Los Angeles Dodgers after recovering from a broken right wrist. Right-handed reliever Adam Ottavino was dropped from the Mets' roster. Tommy Edman will start at shortstop throughout the NLCS, Roberts said.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Billy Crystal and Spike Lee take their places at the Hall of Fame as basketball superfans
item.description: Honored for his devotion to a basketball team that doesn't have a Hall of Fame history, Billy Crystal couldn't help but note the irony. The actor is being added to the Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame’s James F.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bears TE Cole Kmet catches 2 of Caleb Williams' 4 TD passes, handles long snaps in 35-16 rout of Jaguars in London
item.description: WATCH: Jarrett Payton loves what he's seeing from the Bears' offense in the attached video from Monday's WGN Morning News. LONDON (AP) — Chicago Bears tight end Cole Kmet faced his biggest fear in football with no notice. In a snap, you might say.
item.dc:creator: KEN MAGUIRE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: AP Top 25: Oregon, Penn State move behind No. 1 Texas. Army, Navy both ranked for 1st time since '60
item.description: Oregon and Penn State each moved up a spot in The Associated Press college football poll on Sunday following thrilling wins in high-profile games, and Top 25 newcomers Navy and Army are in the rankings together for the first time since 1960.
item.dc:creator: ERIC OLSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Kucherov scores go-ahead goal, two empty-netters to push Lightning past Hurricanes in season opener
item.description: Nikita Kucherov had a third-period hat trick to help the Tampa Bay Lightning beat the Carolina Hurricanes 4-1 in Friday night's season opener for both teams.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 49ers rookie receiver Ricky Pearsall will return to practice for the 1st time since he was shot
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (AP) — San Francisco 49ers rookie receiver Ricky Pearsall is set to return to practice next week for the first time since he was shot in the chest in a robbery attempt nine days before the season opener.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, KRON4
